perturbation.original_text: Each Party shall ensure (i) that all persons mentio ned in Paragraph 1.2.2 above are made aware, prior to any disclosure, of the confidential nature of th e Confidential Information and of the contents of t his Agreement and (ii) [that such persons are bound by confidentially with respect to the Confidential Information as If they were a party to this Agreeme nt.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party shall ensure that all persons mentioned in Paragraph 1.2.2 above are made aware, prior to any disclosure, of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing '(ii) [that such persons are bound by confidentially with respect to the Confidential Information as If they were a party to this Agreeme nt', it removes the legal obligation for those parties to be bound by the confidentiality of the NDA, and now only the party needs to make them aware of it's confidential nature. This is in direct contradiction with 3.1 which holds each party responsible under Danish law for damages arising from their breach of obligations, without the second part of this, there may be no damages if advisors disclose the confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party shall be liable in accordance with Danis h law for damages (including claims, expenses and indirect losses of whatsoever nature) arising out o f or in connection with that Party’s breach of its obligations under this Agreement, without prejudice to other rights and remedies available including, but not limited to, restrictive injunction and other eq uitable relief.
perturbation.justification: The original text ensured that persons mentioned in Paragraph 1.2.2 were not only aware of the confidential information but also bound by confidentiality, similar to a party of the agreement. The changed text removes the obligation for those individuals to be bound by confidentiality. The contradicted text states that each party is liable under Danish law for damages resulting from a breach of their obligations. By removing the confidentiality obligation from those mentioned in paragraph 1.2.2, the changed text creates a contradiction. If those individuals disclose confidential information, and they are not bound by the agreement, there may be no damages for which a party would be liable, creating a contradiction with the liability clause.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties shall have in effect and maintain adequ ate security measures to safeguard the Confidential Information from unauthorized access, disclosure, u se, and/or misappropriation.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties shall endeavor to maintain security measures to safeguard the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing 'have in effect and maintain adequate' with 'endeavor to maintain', the obligation for security measures is significantly weakened from a requirement to a suggestion. This directly affects section 1.4, which states 'A Party shall promptly notify the other Party of, w ithout limitation, any unauthorized access, use, co pying or disclosure of Confidential Information of which a Party becomes aware', if there is no obligation for security measures in place, then it's not clear how unauthorized access, use, copying or disclosure are to be defined, or if failure to prevent it is even a breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A Party shall promptly notify the other Party of, w ithout limitation, any unauthorized access, use, co pying or disclosure of Confidential Information of which a Party becomes aware and shall use its best endeav ors to terminate such access, use etc. and shall provid e all reasonable assistance requested by the other Party in this connection.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated a requirement to 'have in effect and maintain adequate security measures', whereas the changed text weakens this obligation to 'endeavor to maintain security measures'. The contradicted text states that 'A Party shall promptly notify the other Party of any unauthorized access... of Confidential Information', which implies an expectation of security measures being in place to prevent such access. Because the changed text weakens the obligation to have security measures in the first place, it's no longer clear what constitutes 'unauthorized access' or how it would be prevented, thus creating a direct contradiction and uncertainty regarding the enforcement of notification requirements related to unauthorized access as described in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: All right, title and interest (including without li mitation all intellectual property and other propri etary rights) throughout the world in and to any and all Confiden tial Information disclosed by a Party as well as inventions, discoveries, improvements, ideas, conce pts, designs, know-how, data, code, software, produ cts, 3 information, documentation, techniques, materials a nd/or other work product in any form and on whatever media and any enhancements, upgrades, modi fications and/or alterations thereto in any form, created, conceived, prepared, made, developed, orig inated and/or delivered (in whole or in part) by or for a Party shall belong exclusively and absolutely to such Party, its sub-contractors and/or licensors (a s the case may be).
perturbation.changed_text: All right, title and interest to Confidential Information disclosed by a Party shall belong to such Party.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the clause '(including without li mitation all intellectual property and other propri etary rights) throughout the world in and to any and all Confiden tial Information disclosed by a Party as well as inventions, discoveries, improvements, ideas, conce pts, designs, know-how, data, code, software, produ cts, 3 information, documentation, techniques, materials a nd/or other work product in any form and on whatever media and any enhancements, upgrades, modi fications and/or alterations thereto in any form, created, conceived, prepared, made, developed, orig inated and/or delivered (in whole or in part) by or for a Party shall belong exclusively and absolutely to such Party, its sub-contractors and/or licensors (a s the case may be).' Limits the ownership of confidential information to only the Confidential Information disclosed, but not intellectual property rights or improvements to the confidential information, making the scope of ownership unclear in Section 2.2, which states 'Nothing in this Agreement shall grant or be constru ed as granting or conferring to a Party any rights, licenses of whatsoever nature, expressly or implied , in the other Party’s intellectual property and Confidential Information.', because it removes context and scope to what intellectual property is referred to, because it has been removed from the previous section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Nothing in this Agreement shall grant or be constru ed as granting or conferring to a Party any rights, licenses of whatsoever nature, expressly or implied , in the other Party’s intellectual property and Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly states that all intellectual property and other proprietary rights related to the confidential information belong to the disclosing party. The changed text removes this clarification, limiting the ownership to only the confidential information itself. The contradicted text states that nothing in the agreement grants any rights to the other party's intellectual property and confidential information. The original text clearly delineates what is owned, whereas the changed text removes this definition, thus leading to a contradiction and ambiguity as to what exactly is being referred to in the Contradicted Text. Therefore, there's a clear contradiction introduced by the omission, making it unclear what IP is covered in the second section, thus leading to a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party shall be liable in accordance with Danis h law for damages (including claims, expenses and indirect losses of whatsoever nature) arising out o f or in connection with that Party’s breach of its obligations under this Agreement, without prejudice to other rights and remedies available including, but not limited to, restrictive injunction and other eq uitable relief.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party shall be liable for damages arising out of a breach of its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the specification 'in accordance with Danish law' and '(including claims, expenses and indirect losses of whatsoever nature), without prejudice to other rights and remedies available including, but not limited to, restrictive injunction and other eq uitable relief' creates ambiguity regarding the applicable law and what types of damages can be claimed in section 4.1, which states 'This Agreement shall be governed by and construed i n accordance with Danish law', suggesting Danish Law applies, but it removes clarity by removing it from the damages section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed i n accordance with Danish law.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that liability is 'in accordance with Danish law' and includes 'indirect losses of whatsoever nature'. The changed text removes this specification, creating ambiguity about the governing law and types of damages that can be claimed. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text', which explicitly states that 'This Agreement shall be governed by and construed i n accordance with Danish law.' The change removes clarity and creates a potential conflict regarding the applicable law and recoverable damages, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction as stated by the Perturbation Explanation, and aligning with the Omissions - In Text Contradiction definition.
